title: limniris setosa
intro: Limniris setosa, also known as the Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It is native to wetlands and marshy areas in Europe and Asia, and its bright yellow flowers have been used for centuries in herbal medicine. The plant contains alkaloids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Limniris setosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
